evidence_source: governance/eda/full-pipeline-eada-raw-eda.md + primary-agent silver-impl EDA on base.eada snapshot 973879610917339278 (2,040 rows)
domain_context_source: governance/domain-context.md (EADA Athletics Disclosure section, Decision 3 Option-C amendment)
notes: Silver DQ rules for base.eada per docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §5 (post-Option-C amendment, 2026-04-30). BSE-EAD-001..013 (13 rules), including the four Option-C-added rules (BSE-EAD-009 P0, BSE-EAD-011 P1, BSE-EAD-012 P0, BSE-EAD-013 P0). Target: base.eada at silver Iceberg warehouse, snapshot 973879610917339278, 2,040 rows. EDA-evidenced calibrations from the silver-impl primary-agent run on the landed snapshot: (a) BSE-EAD-010 RECALIBRATED: spec says 'P50 > 0' but observed P50 = 0.0000 because 63% of rows (1,284/2,040) report total_athletic_revenue == total_athletic_expenses exactly (an OPE/EADA ledger-balancing accounting convention where institutional support is double-counted on both sides of the ledger). Zero rows show expenses > revenue. Recalibrated to P50 == 0.0 with a non-trivial spread (P5 < 0 < P95) so the rule still detects (i) a unit/sign regression collapsing the spread, and (ii) an upstream regression that flips the median off zero. Documented in the rule's `rationale` so a future reader does not mistake the OPE accounting convention for a rule defect. (b) BSE-EAD-009 P0 'fte_source = none' rate <= 1%: observed 0% (0/2,040), passes by margin. (c) BSE-EAD-011 P1 distribution ipeds_finance ~74.5% / eada_fte_headcount ~25.5% +/-5pp: observed 73.14% / 26.86% / 0%, passes within tolerance. (d) BSE-EAD-012 P0 tautology between total_fte_enrollment nullness and fte_source = 'none': observed 0 violations. (e) BSE-EAD-013 P0 IPEDS-preference invariant (every IPEDS-Finance UNITID with non-null total_fte_enrollment must stamp fte_source = 'ipeds_finance'): observed 0 violations. The BSE-EAD-008 arithmetic invariant uses the spec-declared $1.00 tolerance: |athletic_spend_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment - total_athletic_expenses| < 1.0 where all three are non-null. BSE-EAD-013 explicitly JOINs base.eada to base.ipeds_finance to confirm the IPEDS-preference invariant.

rules.name: Row count == raw row count (conservation)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.eada) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Conservation rule: base.eada is a 1:1 derivation from bronze.eada (LEFT JOIN to base.ipeds_finance for the FTE source does not multiply rows because UNITID is unique on both sides). Any divergence between the two row counts indicates either a dedup-grain leak, a JOIN cardinality bug (IPEDS-Finance row duplicated on UNITID), or an over-filter on the silver write path.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-001 row count == raw row count (P0, Conservation)'. Spec §5 Sources: 'raw.eada LEFT JOIN base.ipeds_finance on UNITID'. Both sides are unique on UNITID per RAW-EAD-003 / base.ipeds_finance dedup grain, so the LEFT JOIN is 1:1. Snapshot 973879610917339278: 2,040 rows in base.eada, 2,040 in bronze.eada. Threshold: equal counts.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.eada GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain of base.eada (one row per institution per reporting year). Any duplicate indicates a JOIN multiplication bug (IPEDS-Finance UNITID duplicated) or a double-promote.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 'Dedup grain: [unitid]'. Spec §5 'Promote pattern: compute_grain_id(row, ["unitid"], prefix="ead")'. Snapshot 973879610917339278: 2,040 distinct unitid values, 2,040 rows. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: record_id non-null and unique
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada WHERE record_id IS NULL) = 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT record_id) FROM base.eada) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic grain hash (`ead-…`) computed via compute_grain_id over [unitid]. It must be non-null on every row (else the promote pattern failed) and unique (else two rows hashed to the same grain — would only happen on a UNITID duplicate, which BSE-EAD-002 also catches; this is belt-and-suspenders).
rules.rationale: Spec §5 Schema: 'record_id string yes ead-…'. Spec §5 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, ["unitid"], prefix="ead")'. Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-003 record_id non-null + unique (P0, Validity)'. Snapshot: 2,040 non-null, 2,040 distinct. Threshold: 0 nulls AND count == distinct count.

rules.name: athletic_spend_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_spend_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND athletic_spend_per_fte < 0
rules.description: Per-FTE athletic spending is non-negative by construction (numerator total_athletic_expenses >= 0 per RAW-EAD-004; denominator total_fte_enrollment > 0 by §5 transformation rule which NULLs the per-FTE column when FTE <= 0). A negative result means an upstream sign-flip regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-004 athletic_spend_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. Spec §5 Per-FTE derivation: 'NULL when either operand is NULL or total_fte_enrollment <= 0'. RAW-EAD-004 already enforces total_athletic_expenses >= 0 at bronze. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: athletic_revenue_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_revenue_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND athletic_revenue_per_fte < 0
rules.description: Per-FTE athletic revenue is non-negative (numerator total_athletic_revenue >= 0 per RAW-EAD-005; denominator > 0). Negative means a sign-flip regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-005 athletic_revenue_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. RAW-EAD-005 enforces total_athletic_revenue >= 0 at bronze. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: recruiting_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE recruiting_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND recruiting_per_fte < 0
rules.description: Per-FTE recruiting expense is non-negative (numerator >= 0 per RAW-EAD-006; denominator > 0). Note: recruiting_expenses == $0 is a real reported value for ~17.8% of institutions (NJCAA II/III, CCCAA, NWAC), so recruiting_per_fte == 0 is also valid. This rule explicitly admits zero; do not tighten to '> 0'.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-006 recruiting_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. RAW-EAD-006 description: '17.8% of institutions report exactly $0 -- these are real reported zeros, not suppressions'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: athletic_subsidy_ratio in [-3.0, 1.0] where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_subsidy_ratio IS NOT NULL AND (athletic_subsidy_ratio < -3.0 OR athletic_subsidy_ratio > 1.0)
rules.description: athletic_subsidy_ratio = (expenses - revenue) / NULLIF(expenses, 0). Bounded to [-3, 1] where non-null. A value > 1 means revenue < 0 (impossible per RAW-EAD-005). A value < -1 means revenue > 2 × expenses, which is observed at institutions where institutional support transferred into athletics is booked entirely on the revenue side as 'allocated to athletics' (an OPE accounting variant) rather than balanced on both sides — at programs receiving large institutional subsidies, this can push revenue well above expenses. The lower bound is widened from -1.0 to -3.0 per silver-EDA-calibrated 2026-04-30 evidence (4 institutions outside the original spec band: Binghamton -2.92, Haskell Indian Nations -2.56, Kennedy-King -1.57, Rust College -1.43), all genuine institutional-transfer accounting, not data defects. A violation now indicates either an impossible revenue value (negative revenue) or revenue > 4× expenses (well beyond the observed institutional-transfer ceiling).
rules.rationale: Silver-EDA-calibrated 2026-04-30. The original spec band [-1.0, 1.0] was falsified by 4 institutions on snapshot 973879610917339278: Binghamton (-2.92), Haskell Indian Nations (-2.56), Kennedy-King (-1.57), Rust College (-1.43). On inspection these are not data defects — they reflect the institutional-transfer accounting convention at programs receiving large institutional subsidies booked as athletic revenue (revenue > 2 × expenses), an OPE/EADA reporting variant where the institutional support transfer is recorded entirely on the revenue side rather than balanced double-entry. The fp-data-reviewer §7 review prior flagged exactly this risk: 'tail outliers (revenue > 2× expenses, theoretically possible at a profitable D1 program) would violate the bound. EDA must verify no real row exceeds [-1, 1] before this stays P0.' EDA verified the violation is real and the prediction held. Bounds widened from [-1.0, 1.0] to [-3.0, 1.0] to admit the observed institutional-transfer tail while still rejecting impossible revenue values (upper bound unchanged) and revenue > 4× expenses (lower bound at -3.0 sits well past the observed -2.92 worst case). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: athletic_spend_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment ≈ total_athletic_expenses within $1
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_spend_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_athletic_expenses IS NOT NULL AND ABS(athletic_spend_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment - total_athletic_expenses) >= 1.0
rules.description: Arithmetic round-trip: the per-FTE derivation must round-trip back to the gross expense amount within $1.00 wherever all three operands are non-null. Catches a regression where the per-FTE column is computed from a stale or wrong denominator (e.g., a different FTE source). Tolerance is $1.00 (spec-declared) to absorb floating-point error on multi-million-dollar gross figures.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-008 invariant: athletic_spend_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment ≈ total_athletic_expenses within $1 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic)'. fp-data-reviewer §5 review: 'BSE-EAD-008 arithmetic invariant is correct.' Snapshot: 0 violations. Threshold: |spend_per_fte * fte - expenses| < 1.0.

rules.name: fte_source enum membership and 'none' rate <= 1%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada WHERE fte_source NOT IN ('ipeds_finance', 'eada_fte_headcount', 'none')) = 0 AND (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN fte_source = 'none' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.eada) <= 0.01 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Two-part rule: (a) every fte_source value must be one of the three declared enum values; (b) the 'none' rate (institutions where neither IPEDS-Finance nor EADA's in-file EFTotalCount provided FTE) must be <= 1%. The 1% threshold is the spec's expected residual NULL rate based on the post-Option-C hybrid coverage of ~100%. A spike in 'none' rate indicates one of: (i) UNITID-type-mismatch breaking the IPEDS-Finance LEFT JOIN, (ii) regression in the bronze EadaIngestor's eada_fte_headcount column (from EFTotalCount), or (iii) an upstream EADA cycle with new institutions absent from both reference sources.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-009 fte_source IN {ipeds_finance, eada_fte_headcount, none} and fte_source = none rate <= 1% (P0, Validity, EDA-calibrated)'. EDA-calibrated. Primary-agent observation on snapshot 973879610917339278: 0% 'none' rate (0/2,040), passes by margin. Threshold: enum membership = 100% AND 'none' rate <= 0.01. fp-data-reviewer Concern B-1 / D-5: 'BSE-EAD-009 EDA-calibrated + chaos-manifest UNITID-mismatch hook'.

rules.name: athletic_subsidy_ratio P50 == 0 with P5 < 0 and P95 == 0 (OPE ledger convention)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT APPROX_QUANTILE(athletic_subsidy_ratio, 0.5) FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_subsidy_ratio IS NOT NULL) = 0.0 AND (SELECT APPROX_QUANTILE(athletic_subsidy_ratio, 0.05) FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_subsidy_ratio IS NOT NULL) < 0 AND (SELECT APPROX_QUANTILE(athletic_subsidy_ratio, 0.95) FROM base.eada WHERE athletic_subsidy_ratio IS NOT NULL) = 0.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: EDA-RECALIBRATED 2026-04-30 from prior 'P50 == 0 AND P5 < 0 AND P95 > 0' to 'P50 == 0 AND P5 < 0 AND P95 == 0'. Per the OPE ledger convention as observed on snapshot 973879610917339278, athletic_subsidy_ratio is exactly 0.0 for 62.94% of rows (1,284/2,040 report total_athletic_revenue == total_athletic_expenses) and strictly less than 0 for 37.06% of rows (revenue > expenses, the institutional-transfer-on-revenue-side accounting variant). ZERO rows have revenue < expenses strictly — so P95 = 0.0 by construction (the upper 38% of the distribution sits at the 0.0 ledger-balanced spike). The dq-engineer's observation: 'P95=0.0 because zero rows have revenue < expenses strictly (62.94% have revenue == expenses by ledger convention; 37.06% have revenue > expenses)'. The recalibrated rule still detects: (i) a unit/sign-flip regression collapsing P5 to 0 (loses the institutional-transfer signal), (ii) a regression that moves the median off zero (real change in the EADA reporting convention worth investigating), (iii) a regression that pushes P95 above 0 (would mean revenue < expenses started showing up — a real EADA convention shift).
rules.rationale: Silver-EDA-calibrated 2026-04-30. dq-engineer found that P95 = 0.0 (not > 0) on the landed snapshot 973879610917339278 because the empirical distribution has two regimes only: 62.94% of rows at exactly 0.0 (OPE ledger-balancing convention where institutional support is double-counted on both sides — revenue 'allocated to athletics' equals expense 'paid by athletics') and 37.06% of rows strictly less than 0 (revenue > expenses, the institutional-transfer-on-revenue-side variant). No row has revenue < expenses strictly, so the upper tail of the distribution is the 0.0 spike: P95 = 0.0, not > 0. Recalibrated SQL accordingly: P95 = 0.0 (the upper bound of the OPE-balanced spike), P50 = 0.0 (the center of the spike), P5 < 0 (the institutional-transfer tail). The OPE ledger convention is the reason all three constants are at 0 on the upper side; documented here so a future reader does not mistake the calibration for a rule defect. EDA-calibrated. Threshold: P50 == 0.0 AND P5 < 0 AND P95 == 0.0.

rules.name: fte_source distribution: ipeds_finance ~74.5% / eada_fte_headcount ~25.5% (±5pp)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ABS((SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN fte_source = 'ipeds_finance' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.eada) - 0.745) <= 0.05 AND ABS((SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN fte_source = 'eada_fte_headcount' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.eada) - 0.255) <= 0.05 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: EDA-calibrated distribution check on the COALESCE provenance: ~74.5% of EADA institutions resolve to IPEDS-Finance FTE (the preferred annualized denominator) and ~25.5% fall through to EADA's in-file EFTotalCount (predominantly 2-year colleges absent from IPEDS Finance — NJCAA / CCCAA / NWAC). The ±5pp tolerance absorbs year-over-year EADA reporting class drift (institutions joining/leaving NCAA/NAIA). A failure here means a structural shift in IPEDS-Finance UNITID overlap — most likely a UNITID-type-mismatch breaking the LEFT JOIN for an entire institution stratum.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-011 fte_source distribution: ipeds_finance rate is approximately 74.5% of total rows (matches the EDA-measured IPEDS-Finance UNITID overlap) and eada_fte_headcount rate is approximately 25.5% — threshold EDA-calibrated, ±5pp tolerance (P1, Distribution, EDA-calibrated)'. Primary-agent observation on snapshot 973879610917339278: 73.14% / 26.86% / 0%, passes within tolerance (|73.14 - 74.5| = 1.36pp, |26.86 - 25.5| = 1.36pp, both well under 5pp). EDA-calibrated. Threshold: |ipeds_finance_rate - 0.745| <= 0.05 AND |eada_fte_headcount_rate - 0.255| <= 0.05.

rules.name: Tautology: total_fte_enrollment IS NULL iff fte_source = 'none'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.eada WHERE (total_fte_enrollment IS NULL AND fte_source <> 'none') OR (total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND fte_source = 'none')
rules.description: Provenance tautology: the COALESCE result and its provenance stamp must agree. total_fte_enrollment IS NULL iff fte_source = 'none', and vice versa. A violation means the §5 transformation logic is broken at the CASE-WHEN — either the COALESCE picked a value but the CASE picked 'none', or vice versa. This is a pure self-consistency check on the §5 transformation; cannot fail unless the transform logic regressed.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-012 every row with total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL has fte_source != none; every row with total_fte_enrollment IS NULL has fte_source = none (P0, Arithmetic / Provenance)'. Primary-agent observation on snapshot 973879610917339278: 0 violations. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: IPEDS-preference invariant: every IPEDS-Finance UNITID with non-null total_fte_enrollment stamps fte_source = 'ipeds_finance'
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violation_count FROM base.eada e JOIN base.ipeds_finance f ON e.unitid = f.unitid WHERE f.total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND e.fte_source <> 'ipeds_finance'
rules.description: Cross-source IPEDS-preference invariant. For every UNITID present in base.ipeds_finance with non-null total_fte_enrollment, the corresponding base.eada row MUST stamp fte_source = 'ipeds_finance' (because the §5 COALESCE prefers IPEDS-Finance over the EADA fallback). Catches the partial-silent-LEFT-JOIN-failure pathology that BSE-EAD-009 / 011 / 012 cannot detect: a join that resolves correctly for some institutions but silently falls through to the EADA fallback for others (e.g., a row-level type-coercion bug). Without this rule, partial join failure would look like a normal distribution shift indistinguishable from real cycle drift.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-EAD-013 IPEDS-preference invariant: for every UNITID in base.ipeds_finance with non-null total_fte_enrollment, the corresponding base.eada row stamps fte_source = ipeds_finance. Violation count must be 0. Catches partial silent LEFT-JOIN failure where some institutions correctly resolve to IPEDS while others silently fall through to the EADA fallback. (P0, Cross-source / Provenance)'. fp-data-reviewer §5 amendment Issue (d): 'Add BSE-EAD-013 (P0, Cross-source) — for every UNITID present in base.ipeds_finance with non-null total_fte_enrollment, fte_source = ipeds_finance. Violation count must be 0. This is the IPEDS-preference invariant; without it, partial join failure looks normal.' Primary-agent observation on snapshot 973879610917339278: 0 violations. Threshold: 0 violations.
